title: Lemon Garlic Prawns
description: My Thermomix take on Rick Stein's garlic prawns — buttery, lemony and quick, tossed with parsley.
intro: This is my independent Thermomix homage to Rick Stein and the comforting appeal of garlic prawns. It keeps the spirit of the original inspiration, but the method is written fresh for TM6 and TM7 cooks who want clear settings and a warm, achievable result.
steps: Chop garlic 3 sec / speed 6. Add butter, oil, lemon zest and chilli, sauté 3 min / 120°C / speed 1.
Add the prawns and cook 6 min / 100°C / Reverse / speed 1, until just pink.
Finish with lemon juice, salt and parsley. Serve with bread to mop the butter.
tips: Don't overcook — prawns are done the moment they turn pink.
A splash of white wine at step 1 is lovely.

faq.a: Thaw and pat dry first so they sear rather than stew.

faq.q: Is this the original Rick Stein recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Rick Stein and garlic prawns; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
